parsed.expected_effect: The rule will now trigger a notification when a git commit occurs after a bash script has written to `kubernetes/apis/**` without a subsequent `make` command.
parsed.policy_yaml:
# directive: "After writing Kubernetes API type definitions under kubernetes/apis/, run the Kubernetes generation command so CRD manifests and DeepCopy outputs stay in sync."
# repo: "alibaba__OpenSandbox"
# statement_id: "kubernetes_apis_make_manifests_generate"
# enforceability: "cross_event"
# context_required: "project"
# notes: "A commit after Kubernetes API changes requires a prior manifest
#   generation step. This relies on write-before-commit temporal state."
version: 1
policy: |
  source AGENT = exec "python3"
  source AGENT_ALT = exec "python"
  source AGENT_BASH = exec "bash"

  rule kubernetes_apis_make_manifests_generate:
    notify exec "git" "commit" if AGENT or AGENT_ALT or AGENT_BASH unless after exec "make" since write "kubernetes/apis/**"
    because "After writing Kubernetes API type definitions under kubernetes/apis/, run the Kubernetes generation command so CRD manifests and DeepCopy outputs stay in sync."
parsed.rationale: The original rule only tracked writes initiated by Python agents. The false-negative trace shows a violation where a bash script (executed via `bash .eval-fixtures/task.sh`) wrote to `kubernetes/apis/`. By adding `exec "bash"` as a source agent, the policy now detects writes performed by bash scripts, ensuring the `unless after exec "make"` condition is evaluated for these opaque fixture executions.

{
  "status": "revised",
  "policy_yaml": "# directive: "After writing Kubernetes API type definitions under kubernetes/apis/, run the Kubernetes generation command so CRD manifests and DeepCopy outputs stay in sync."
# repo: "alibaba__OpenSandbox"
# statement_id: "kubernetes_apis_make_manifests_generate"
# enforceability: "cross_event"
# context_required: "project"
# notes: "A commit after Kubernetes API changes requires a prior manifest
#   generation step. This relies on write-before-commit temporal state."
version: 1
policy: |
  source AGENT = exec "python3"
  source AGENT_ALT = exec "python"
  source AGENT_BASH = exec "bash"

  rule kubernetes_apis_make_manifests_generate:
    notify exec "git" "commit" if AGENT or AGENT_ALT or AGENT_BASH unless after exec "make" since write "kubernetes/apis/**"
    because "After writing Kubernetes API type definitions under kubernetes/apis/, run the Kubernetes generation command so CRD manifests and DeepCopy outputs stay in sync."
",
  "rationale": "The original rule only tracked writes initiated by Python agents. The false-negative trace shows a violation where a bash script (executed via `bash .eval-fixtures/task.sh`) wrote to `kubernetes/apis/`. By adding `exec "bash"` as a source agent, the policy now detects writes performed by bash scripts, ensuring the `unless after exec "make"` condition is evaluated for these opaque fixture executions.",
  "expected_effect": "The rule will now trigger a notification when a git commit occurs after a bash script has written to `kubernetes/apis/**` without a subsequent `make` command."
}
